result:
Done. The API is built, verified end-to-end, and committed.

## What was built

A dependency-free Node.js (ESM) JSON REST API:

- **`server.js`** — raw `node:http` server (no Express/framework), routes requests, parses/validates JSON bodies, enforces bearer-token auth.
- **`db.js`** — persistent on-disk store using the built-in `node:sqlite` (`data.db` WAL file). Handles users, sessions, and notes; passwords are salted+hashed with scrypt and verified in constant time.

## Endpoints

| Method | Path | Auth | Behavior |
|--------|------|------|----------|
| POST | `/signup` | — | `{username, password}` → `201 {id, username}` (409 if taken) |
| POST | `/login` | — | `{username, password}` → `200 {session}` (401 if wrong) |
| GET | `/me` | Bearer | `{id, username}` |
| POST | `/notes` | Bearer | `{title, body}` → `201` created note |
| GET | `/notes` | Bearer | array of the caller's notes |

Bad requests return sensible codes (400 invalid/missing fields, 401 unauthenticated, 404 unknown route, 413 oversized body). Notes are scoped per-user (verified bob sees `[]` while alice has hers). Port comes from `PORT` (default 3000).

## Verification
Ran the full flow live: signup, duplicate rejection, login, auth enforcement, note creation, per-user isolation, invalid-JSON handling — then **killed and restarted the server** and confirmed users and notes persisted. `npm start` runs it; `data.db*` files are gitignored.
